summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding the Azure PostgreSQL resources to the application model.

members.name: AddAzurePostgresFlexibleServer
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzurePostgresFlexibleServerResource> AzurePostgresExtensions.AddAzurePostgresFlexibleServer(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name)

summary.text: Adds an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource to the application model.

remarks.text: By default, the Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource is configured to use Microsoft Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) for authentication. This requires changes to the application code to use an azure credential to authenticate with the resource. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/postgresql/flexible-server/how-to-connect-with-managed-identity#connect-using-managed-identity-in-c for more information. You can use the
method to configure the resource to use password authentication.
The following example creates an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource and referencing that resource in a .NET project.

builder.text: The builder for the distributed application.

members.name: AddDatabase
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzurePostgresFlexibleServerDatabaseResource> AzurePostgresExtensions.AddDatabase(this IResourceBuilder<AzurePostgresFlexibleServerResource> builder, string name, string? databaseName = null)

summary.text: Adds an Azure PostgreSQL database to the application model.

builder.text: The Azure PostgreSQL server resource builder.

databaseName.text: The name of the database. If not provided, this defaults to the same value as

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

attributes.name: System.ObsoleteAttribute
constructorArguments: This method is obsolete and will be removed in a future version. Use AddAzurePostgresFlexibleServer instead to add an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource.

summary.text: Configures resource to use Azure for local development and when doing a deployment via the Azure Developer CLI.

attributes.name: System.ObsoleteAttribute
constructorArguments: This method is obsolete and will be removed in a future version. Use AddAzurePostgresFlexibleServer instead to add an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource.

summary.text: Configures Postgres Server resource to be deployed as Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
Configures an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource to run locally in a container.

remarks.text: The following example creates an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource that runs locally in a PostgreSQL container and referencing that resource in a .NET project.

builder.text: The Azure PostgreSQL server resource builder.

configureContainer.text: Callback that exposes underlying container to allow for customization.

members.name: WithPasswordAuthentication
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzurePostgresFlexibleServerResource> AzurePostgresExtensions.WithPasswordAuthentication(this IResourceBuilder<AzurePostgresFlexibleServerResource> builder, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? userName = null, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? password = null)

summary.text: Configures the resource to use password authentication for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server.

remarks.text: The following example creates an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server resource that uses password authentication.

builder.text: The Azure PostgreSQL server resource builder.

password.text: The parameter used to provide the administrator password for the PostgreSQL resource. If
a random password will be generated.

userName.text: The parameter used to provide the user name for the PostgreSQL resource. If
a default value will be used.

members.name: WithPasswordAuthentication
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzurePostgresFlexibleServerResource> AzurePostgresExtensions.WithPasswordAuthentication(this IResourceBuilder<AzurePostgresFlexibleServerResource> builder, IResourceBuilder<IAzureKeyVaultResource> keyVaultBuilder, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? userName = null, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? password = null)

summary.text: Configures the resource to use password authentication for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server. This overload is used when the PostgreSQL resource is created in a container and the password is stored in an Azure Key Vault secret.

builder.text: The Azure PostgreSQL server resource builder.

keyVaultBuilder.text: The Azure Key Vault resource builder.

password.text: The parameter used to provide the administrator password for the PostgreSQL resource. If
a random password will be generated.

userName.text: The parameter used to provide the user name for the PostgreSQL resource. If
a default value will be used.

members.name: WithPostgresMcp

summary.text: Adds a Postgres MCP server container and configures it to connect to the database represented by

children.text: The Postgres MCP server is configured to use SSE transport and will expose an HTTP endpoint.
This extension method only applies when the Azure PostgreSQL resource is running as a container (i.e.,
). If the resource is not running as a container, this method has no effect.

builder.text: The Azure Postgres database resource builder.

configureContainer.text: Configuration callback for the Postgres MCP container resource.

members.signature: public AzurePostgresFlexibleServerDatabaseResource.AzurePostgresFlexibleServerDatabaseResource(string name, string databaseName, AzurePostgresFlexibleServerResource postgresParentResource)

summary.text: A resource that represents an Azure PostgreSQL database. This is a child resource of an

postgresParentResource.text: The Azure PostgreSQL parent resource associated with this database.

summary.text: Gets the connection string expression for the Postgres database.
Gets the database name.
Gets a value indicating whether the current resource represents a container. If so the actual resource is not running in Azure.
Gets the JDBC connection string for the Azure Postgres Flexible Server database.
Gets the parent Azure PostgresSQL resource.
Gets the connection URI expression for the PostgreSQL server.
Represents an resource for Azure Postgres Flexible Server.
Represents an resource for Azure Postgres Flexible Server.
Gets the connection template for the manifest for the Azure Postgres Flexible Server.
A dictionary where the key is the resource name and the value is the database name.
Gets the host for the PostgreSQL server.

remarks.text: In container mode, resolves to the container's primary endpoint host. In Azure mode, resolves to the Azure PostgreSQL server's fully qualified domain name.

summary.text: Gets the host name for the PostgreSQL server.

remarks.text: In container mode, resolves to the container's primary endpoint host. In Azure mode, resolves to the Azure PostgreSQL server's fully qualified domain name.

summary.text: Gets the "id" output reference for the resource.
Gets a value indicating whether the current resource represents a container. If so the actual resource is not running in Azure.
Gets the JDBC connection string for the server.
Gets the "name" output reference for the resource.
Gets the password for the PostgreSQL server when password authentication is enabled.

remarks.text: This property returns null when using Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) authentication. When password authentication is enabled, it resolves to the password parameter value.

summary.text: Gets the port for the PostgreSQL server.

remarks.text: In container mode, resolves to the container's primary endpoint port. In Azure mode, resolves to 5432.

summary.text: Gets the connection URI expression for the PostgreSQL server.
Gets a value indicating whether the resource uses password authentication.
Gets the user name for the PostgreSQL server when password authentication is enabled.

remarks.text: This property returns null when using Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) authentication. When password authentication is enabled, it resolves to the user name parameter value.
